title: Roasted Japanese Tofu Gyoza
description: A flaky, nostalgic Japanese dish featuring almond extract, deep-fried tofu, and a side of tangy dipping sauce. Perfect for a quick weeknight meal or a special occasion.
instructions: Cut the tofu into small cubes and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the tofu with 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and toss to coat.
Bake the tofu in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and almond extract. Mix well to combine.
Add the soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, water, and sugar to the mixing bowl. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 20-24 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball of dough into a disk shape, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a piece of fried tofu in the center of each dough disk.
Fold the dough over the tofu to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Heat about 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the gyoza to the skillet and cook until the bottoms are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Flip the gyoza and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also golden brown.
Serve the gyoza hot with a side of tangy dipping sauce, made by mixing together equal parts soy sauce and rice vinegar.
Garnish with sliced scallions and serve immediately.
Serve the gyoza with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad for a well-rounded meal.
If desired, add additional toppings such as diced green onions, grated carrot, or sliced mushrooms.
To make the dipping sauce, combine equal parts soy sauce and rice vinegar in a small bowl. Mix well to combine.
Taste and adjust the dipping sauce as needed, adding more soy sauce or rice vinegar to taste.
Strain the dipping sauce through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any excess oil or sediment.
Serve the dipping sauce chilled or at room temperature, garnished with sliced scallions if desired.
